data.title: Kind of Blue
data.label: Columbia Records
data.summary: The best-selling jazz album of all time, a landmark in modal jazz that redefined the genre.
data.liner_notes: Recorded in two sessions in March and April 1959, *Kind of Blue* was conceived almost entirely through Miles Davis's modal approach — scales rather than chord changes as the basis for improvisation. The album's unhurried tempos and sparse arrangements gave the musicians unprecedented freedom, and the result was a quiet revolution. Evans's impressionistic comping on *So What* and Coltrane's searching lines on *Flamenco Sketches* remain definitive moments in jazz history.
